name: SIGMA - Windows WebDAV User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects WebDav DownloadCradle
false_positives: Administrative scripts that download files from the Internet
Administrative scripts that retrieve certain website contents

name: SIGMA - Download from Suspicious Dyndns Hosts
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects download of certain file types from hosts with dynamic DNS names (selected list)

name: SIGMA - Download From Suspicious TLD - Blacklist
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects download of certain file types from hosts in suspicious TLDs

name: SIGMA - Download From Suspicious TLD - Whitelist
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects executable downloads from suspicious remote systems

name: SIGMA - F5 BIG-IP iControl Rest API Command Execution - Proxy
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Thurein Oo
description: Detects POST requests to the F5 BIG-IP iControl Rest API "bash" endpoint, which allows the execution of commands on the BIG-IP
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the BIG IP REST API to execute command for administration purposes

name: SIGMA - Potential Hello-World Scraper Botnet Activity
author: Joseph A. M.
description: Detects network traffic potentially associated with a scraper botnet variant that uses the "Hello-World/1.0" user-agent string.
false_positives: Legitimate network monitoring or vulnerability scanning tools that may use this generic user agent.
Internal development or testing scripts. Consider filtering by source IP if this is expected from certain systems.

name: SIGMA - HackTool - BabyShark Agent Default URL Pattern
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Baby Shark C2 Framework default communication patterns

name: SIGMA - HackTool - CobaltStrike Malleable Profile Patterns - Proxy
author: Markus Neis, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects cobalt strike malleable profiles patterns (URI, User-Agents, Methods).
query: any where ((c-useragent:"Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko" and cs-method:"GET" and c-uri:"/s/ref=nb_sb_noss_1/167-3294888-0262949/field-keywords=books" and cs-host:"www.amazon.com" and cs-cookie:"*=csm-hit=s-24KU11BB82RZSYGJ3BDK|1419899012996") or (c-useragent:"Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko" and cs-method:"POST" and c-uri:"/N4215/adj/amzn.us.sr.aps" and cs-host:"www.amazon.com") or (c-useragent like~ ("Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 6.0;Windows NT 5.1)", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 7.0; Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/4.0; SLCC2; .NET CLR 3.0.30729; .NET4.0C; .NET4.0E )", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows; U; MSIE 7.0; Windows NT 5.2) Java/1.5.0_08")) or c-useragent:"*; MANM; MANM)" or (c-uri:"*/oscp/*" and cs-host:"ocsp.verisign.com") or (cs-method:"GET" and c-uri:"*\?manifest=wac" and cs-host:"onedrive.live.com")) and (not (c-uri:"http*" and c-uri:"*://onedrive.live.com/*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Empire UserAgent URI Combo
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects user agent and URI paths used by empire agents
false_positives: Valid requests with this exact user agent to server scripts of the defined names
query: any where c-useragent:"Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko" and (cs-uri like~ ("/admin/get.php", "/news.php", "/login/process.php")) and cs-method:"POST"

name: SIGMA - PUA - Advanced IP/Port Scanner Update Check
author: Axel Olsson
description: Detect the update check performed by Advanced IP/Port Scanner utilities.
false_positives: Expected if you legitimately use the Advanced IP or Port Scanner utilities in your environement.
query: any where c-uri:"*/checkupdate.php*" and (c-uri-query:"*lng=*" and c-uri-query:"*ver=*" and c-uri-query:"*beta=*" and c-uri-query:"*type=*" and c-uri-query:"*rmode=*" and c-uri-query:"*product=*")

name: SIGMA - PwnDrp Access
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects downloads from PwnDrp web servers developed for red team testing and most likely also used for criminal activity

name: SIGMA - Raw Paste Service Access
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects direct access to raw pastes in different paste services often used by malware in their second stages to download malicious code in encrypted or encoded form
false_positives: User activity (e.g. developer that shared and copied code snippets and used the raw link instead of just copy & paste)

name: SIGMA - Flash Player Update from Suspicious Location
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a flashplayer update from an unofficial location

subtechnique.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Network Communication With IPFS
author: Gavin Knapp
description: Detects connections to interplanetary file system (IPFS) containing a user's email address which mirrors behaviours observed in recent phishing campaigns leveraging IPFS to host credential harvesting webpages.
false_positives: Legitimate use of IPFS being used in the organisation. However the cs-uri regex looking for a user email will likely negate this.

name: SIGMA - Telegram API Access
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious requests to Telegram API without the usual Telegram User-Agent
false_positives: Legitimate use of Telegram bots in the company

name: SIGMA - APT User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis
description: Detects suspicious user agent strings used in APT malware in proxy logs
query: any where c-useragent like~ ("SJZJ (compatible; MSIE 6.0; Win32)", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.; WOW64; rv:20.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/20.0", "User-Agent: Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 8.0; Windows NT 6.1; name: SIGMA - Suspicious Base64 Encoded User-Agent
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious encoded User-Agent strings, as seen used by some malware.

name: SIGMA - Bitsadmin to Uncommon IP Server Address
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Bitsadmin connections to IP addresses instead of FQDN names
query: any where c-useragent:"Microsoft BITS/*" and (cs-host like~ ("*1", "*2", "*3", "*4", "*5", "*6", "*7", "*8", "*9"))

name: SIGMA - Bitsadmin to Uncommon TLD
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton
description: Detects Bitsadmin connections to domains with uncommon TLDs
false_positives: Rare programs that use Bitsadmin and update from regional TLDs e.g. .uk or .ca
query: any where c-useragent:"Microsoft BITS/*" and (not (cs-host like~ ("*.com", "*.net", "*.org", "*.scdn.co", "*.sfx.ms")))

name: SIGMA - Crypto Miner User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious user agent strings used by crypto miners in proxy logs

name: SIGMA - HTTP Request With Empty User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a potentially suspicious empty user agent strings in proxy log.
Could potentially indicate an uncommon request method.

name: SIGMA - Exploit Framework User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious user agent strings used by exploit / pentest frameworks like Metasploit in proxy logs
query: any where c-useragent like~ ("Internet Explorer *", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 6.0; Windows NT 5.1; SV1; InfoPath.2)", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; Metasploit RSPEC)", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 6.1; Windows NT)", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 6.0; Windows NT 5.1)", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 7.0; Windows NT 6.0)", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 8.0; Windows NT 6.0; Trident/4.0)", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 7.0; Windows NT 6.0; Trident/4.0; SIMBAR={7DB0F6DE-8DE7-4841-9084-28FA914B0F2E}; SLCC1; .N", "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; Googlebot/2.1; +http://www.google.com/bot.html)", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows; U; Windows NT 5.1; en-US) AppleWebKit/525.13 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/4.0.221.6 Safari/525.13", "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0; Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/5.0; MAAU)", "Mozilla/5.0", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; SPIPE/1.0", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.3; rv:39.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/35.0", "Sametime Community Agent", "X-FORWARDED-FOR", "DotDotPwn v2.1", "SIPDROID", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win32; x32; rv:60.0)", "Mozilla/6.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:24.0) Gecko/20140205     Firefox/27.0 Iceweasel/25.3.0", "*wordpress hash grabber*", "*exploit*", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/96.0.4664.110 Safari/537.36")

name: SIGMA - Hack Tool User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious user agent strings user by hack tools in proxy logs
query: any where c-useragent like~ ("*(hydra)*", "* arachni/*", "* BFAC *", "* brutus *", "* cgichk *", "*core-project/1.0*", "* crimscanner/*", "*datacha0s*", "*dirbuster*", "*domino hunter*", "*dotdotpwn*", "*FHScan Core*", "*floodgate*", "*get-minimal*", "*gootkit auto-rooter scanner*", "*grendel-scan*", "* inspath *", "*internet ninja*", "*jaascois*", "* zmeu *", "*masscan*", "* metis *", "*morfeus fucking scanner*", "*n-stealth*", "*nsauditor*", "*pmafind*", "*security scan*", "*springenwerk*", "*teh forest lobster*", "*toata dragostea*", "* vega/*", "*voideye*", "*webshag*", "*webvulnscan*", "* whcc/*", "* Havij*", "*absinthe*", "*bsqlbf*", "*mysqloit*", "*pangolin*", "*sql power injector*", "*sqlmap*", "*sqlninja*", "*uil2pn*", "*ruler*", "*Mozilla/5.0 (Windows; U; Windows NT 5.1; pt-PT; rv:1.9.1.2) Gecko/20090729 Firefox/3.5.2 (.NET CLR 3.5.30729)*")

description: Detects suspicious user agent strings used by malware in proxy logs

name: SIGMA - Windows PowerShell User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Windows PowerShell Web Access
false_positives: Administrative scripts that download files from the Internet
Administrative scripts that retrieve certain website contents

name: SIGMA - Rclone Activity via Proxy
author: Janantha Marasinghe
description: Detects the use of rclone, a command-line program to manage files on cloud storage, via its default user-agent string
false_positives: Valid requests with this exact user agent to that is used by legitimate scripts or sysadmin operations

name: SIGMA - Suspicious User Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious malformed user agent strings in proxy logs
query: any where ((c-useragent like~ ("user-agent*", "Mozilla/3.0 *", "Mozilla/2.0 *", "Mozilla/1.0 *", "Mozilla *", " Mozilla/*", "Mozila/*", "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 6.0; MS Web Services Client Protocol*")) or (c-useragent like~ ("* (compatible;MSIE *", "*.0;Windows NT *", "*loader*")) or (c-useragent like~ ("_", "CertUtil URL Agent", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:60.0)", "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.3; WOW64; rv:28.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/28.0", "HTTPS", "Erbium-UA-4ce7c27cb4be9d32e333bf032c88235a", "x", "xxx"))) and (not (c-useragent:"Mozilla/3.0 * Acrobat *" or (cs-host like~ ("*.acrobat.com", "*.adobe.com", "*.adobe.io"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Base64 Encoded User-Agent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Brian Ingram (update)
description: Detects User Agent strings that end with an equal sign, which can be a sign of base64 encoding.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious External WebDAV Execution
author: Ahmed Farouk
description: Detects executables launched from external WebDAV shares using the WebDAV Explorer integration, commonly seen in initial access campaigns.
query: any where ((c-useragent:"Microsoft-WebDAV-MiniRedir/*" and cs-method:"GET") and (c-uri like~ ("*.7z", "*.bat", "*.dat", "*.cmd", "*.exe", "*.js", "*.lnk", "*.ps1", "*.rar", "*.url", "*.vbe", "*.vbs", "*.zip"))) and (not (cidrMatch(dst_ip, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(dst_ip, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(dst_ip, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(dst_ip, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(dst_ip, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(dst_ip, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(dst_ip, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(dst_ip, "fc00::/7")))
